column5: Brief Description

column4: Federated File System Encoding at the Edge in Smart Cities MPU-based
column5: The Edge devices present a FileSystem not accesible, but only after the boot process and entering the at-the Edge Federation, asking to the neighbours will be able to mount the secure partition. In Smart Cities scenarios the device asks to the federated poles how to decrypt it. Here MPU (Raspberry) devices are considered leveraging Docker Container-based System.

column4: The X3DH Protocol in MCU devices  in TOIT language Based
column5: The X3DH Protocol is adopted when all parties are not online at the same moment. Many use-cases require this security capabiliy. Implementing the protocol in MCU (ESP32) devices in TOIT Language

column4: The X3DH Protocol in MPU devices in GO language Based

column4: Secure Proximity Activations MPU-based
column5: Entering a Location with a SmartPhone and/or a SmartCar, and/or a SmartByke may alert a Bluetooth/WiFi Device and automatically actuating gates, lights, alarms, etc. Leveraging simple MAC Addresses is not secure at all. A new advertisement system should be adopted. Here MPU (Raspberry) devices are considered leveraging Docker Container-based System.
